seo_title: Stabilizing Recurrent Dynamics for Test-Time Scalable Latent…
meta_description: A new training framework, STARS, is proposed to stabilize recurrent dynamics and improve test-time scaling in looped language models.
body_html:
Looped Language Models (LoopLMs) enable efficient latent reasoning through depth recurrence, yet exhibit unreliable test-time scaling behavior.

Current Limitations of LoopLMs

  • Performance often peaks at a certain iteration depth and then collapses with further recurrence.
  • Existing architectures and strategies exhibit an inherent trade-off between stability and effectiveness.

Proposed Solution: STARS (STAbility-driven Recurrent Scaling)

Converges toward stable fixed points while preserving effectiveness represents a promising way to stabilize recurrent dynamics.

STARS constrains latent states to approach asymptotically stable fixed points via efficient Jacobian Spectral Radius Regularization with random loop sampling.

Experimental Results

TaskPerformance on Test-Time Scaling
Arithmetic TasksReliable test-time scaling achieved by STARS.
Complex Mathematical ReasoningSubstantial mitigation of performance degradation as recurrence depth increases, while improving peak performance.

source.title: Stabilizing Recurrent Dynamics for Test-Time Scalable Latent Reasoning in Looped Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new training framework called STARS (STAbility-driven Recurrent Scaling) to improve the scalability of Looped Language Models. The framework constrains latent states to approach asymptotically stable fixed points, enabling reliable test-time scaling and mitigating performance degradation as recurrence depth increases. This approach is based on uncertainty reduction and uses efficient Jacobian Spectral Radius Regularization with random loop sampling. Experiments have shown promising results in both arithmetic tasks and complex mathematical reasoning.
